---
title: "Lecture 6: HTML Hyperlinks & Tables"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 3, 2026"
code-annotations: below
format: 
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    code-link: true
    highlight-style: a11y
    chalkboard: true
    transition: slide
    transition-speed: default
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    theme: 
      - ../course-slides-styles.scss
---

## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 6]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[HTML Hyperlinks & Tables]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 2 | Unit I: HTML Forms & CSS Introduction<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Create different types of hyperlinks
- Understand internal vs external links
- Build and format HTML tables
- Use semantic table elements
- Implement accessibility best practices
:::

::: {.notes}
Links are what make the web interconnected. Tables organize data. Both are essential for web structure.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Hyperlinks Basics"}

[The Anchor Element `<a>`]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Hyperlinks connect web pages and resources:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1"}
<a href="https://example.com">Click Here</a>
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
**Key attributes:**
- `href`: URL to navigate to
- `title`: Tooltip text
- `target`: Where to open (_blank for new tab)
- `rel`: Relationship to target
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[The text between `<a>` and `</a>` is what users see]{.teal-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Types of Links"}

[Four Types of Links]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
[**1. External Links**]{.teal-text}

```html
<a href="https://google.com">
  Google
</a>
```

Full URL to another website
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
[**2. Internal Links**]{.teal-text}

```html
<a href="about.html">
  About
</a>
```

Link to page in same site
:::
:::

. . .

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
[**3. Anchor Links**]{.teal-text}

```html
<a href="#section2">
  Jump to Section
</a>
```

Link to specific section
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
[**4. Email Links**]{.teal-text}

```html
<a href="mailto:test@example.com">
  Email Us
</a>
```

Send email directly
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="External Links"}

[External Links (Absolute URLs)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Link to other websites with full URLs:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2"}
<a href="https://www.example.com">Visit Example</a>

<a href="https://github.com" target="_blank">GitHub (New Tab)</a>
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
**Key points:**
- Must start with `https://` or `http://`
- Use `target="_blank"` to open in new tab
- Consider using `rel="noopener noreferrer"` for security
:::

::: {.fragment}
```html
<a href="https://github.com" 
   target="_blank" 
   rel="noopener noreferrer">GitHub</a>
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Internal Links"}

[Internal Links (Relative URLs)]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

Link to pages within the same website:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
<a href="about.html">About Page</a>

<a href="pages/contact.html">Contact</a>

<a href="../index.html">Home</a>
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
- Use relative paths (no `https://`)
- Goes to other HTML files in project
- Paths relative to current file location
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Anchor Links"}

[Anchor Links (Jump within Page)]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

Link to specific sections on page:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1-2|3|4-5|6-7"}
<!-- At the top, create navigation -->
<nav>
    <a href="#services">Services</a>
    <a href="#team">Team</a>
    <a href="#contact">Contact</a>
</nav>

<!-- Later on page, create sections -->
<h2 id="services">Our Services</h2>
<h2 id="team">Our Team</h2>
<h2 id="contact">Contact Us</h2>
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Clicking "Services" jumps directly to that section]{.magenta-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Email Links"}

[Email Links]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Allow users to send emails directly:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1"}
<a href="mailto:contact@example.com">Email Us</a>
```

**Advanced email link with subject:**

```html
<a href="mailto:contact@example.com?subject=Question">
  Send Question
</a>
```

**With subject and body:**

```html
<a href="mailto:contact@example.com?subject=Support&body=Please help with...">
  Contact Support
</a>
```

---

## {data-menu-title="HTML Tables"}

[Creating Tables]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Tables organize data in rows and columns:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2|3-4|5|6"}
<table border="1">
    <tr>
        <td>Cell 1</td>
        <td>Cell 2</td>
    </tr>
</table>
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
**Elements:**
- `<table>`: Container
- `<tr>`: Table row
- `<td>`: Table data cell
- `<th>`: Table header cell
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Table Structure"}

[Complete Table Structure]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2-4|5-10|11"}
<table border="1">
    <thead>
        <tr>
            <th>Name</th>
            <th>Age</th>
            <th>City</th>
        </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
        <tr>
            <td>Alice</td>
            <td>25</td>
            <td>New York</td>
        </tr>
    </tbody>
</table>
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .topbr}
- `<thead>`: Header rows
- `<tbody>`: Data rows
- `<tfoot>`: Footer rows (optional)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Table Example"}

[Table Example: Student Grades]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.html code-line-numbers="|2-6|7-13"}
<table border="1">
    <thead>
        <tr>
            <th>Student</th>
            <th>Math</th>
            <th>Science</th>
        </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
        <tr>
            <td>John</td>
            <td>85</td>
            <td>90</td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
            <td>Sarah</td>
            <td>92</td>
            <td>88</td>
        </tr>
    </tbody>
</table>
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Table Best Practices"}

[Table Best Practices]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
1. **Use semantic structure** - `<thead>`, `<tbody>`, `<tfoot>`
2. **Header cells** - Use `<th>` for headers, not `<td>`
3. **Captions** - Add `<caption>` above table
4. **Scope attribute** - Help accessibility: `scope="col"`
5. **Keep simple** - Avoid super complex nested tables
6. **CSS for styling** - Don't use `border` attribute, use CSS
7. **Responsive** - On mobile, consider different layouts
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Navigation Example"}

[Building Navigation with Links]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2-5|6"}
<nav>
    <a href="index.html">Home</a>
    <a href="about.html">About</a>
    <a href="services.html">Services</a>
    <a href="contact.html">Contact</a>
</nav>
```

::: {.fragment}
**With styling (later in CSS):**
```css
nav a {
    margin: 10px;
    text-decoration: none;
    color: #333;
}
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Resources"}

[Resources & References]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- [MDN: Links](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a)
- [MDN: Tables](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/table)
- [Accessible Tables](https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/tables/)
- [Course content: Week 2](/content/week-02.qmd)
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 7 - HTML Forms & Form Elements
</div>
</div>
